page: Amazon Web Services m8a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps, but does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8a.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics. Memory performance is strong, with cached small-block read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier, while uncached large-block read bandwidth falls within the top 25%. The server also achieves top-tier results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. For machine learning workloads, CPU-based LLM inference speeds for text generation range from top-tier for small models to strong for medium and large models.

The instance offers a balanced resource profile, delivering high compute density and fast DDR5 memory speeds without the added cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. The baseline network speed of 22 Gbps is moderate compared to higher-tier instances. This server is suited for compute-heavy general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, offering a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the instance achieves top-tier status in single-core and multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving. It also shows strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference and memory write bandwidth. The absence of local NVMe storage represents a key tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for data-intensive tasks. This server is suited for enterprise application hosting, software compilation, database management, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier CPU benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC general-purpose server featuring forty-eight cores and fast DDR5 memory for balanced, high-throughput compute workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier CPU, database, and static web serving benchmark performance
Nitro hypervisor with 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
